Item 2. Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations

Data Center revenue surpassed prior records as Blackwell architecture products entered volume production, extending strong demand from hyperscale cloud providers and enterprise customers building artificial intelligence infrastructure beyond the Hopper generation.

Gaming revenue grew 12 percent year-over-year, moderating from earlier quarters as the refresh cycle aged, though desktop and laptop attach rates for our discrete GPU generation remained above prior launch levels, supporting sustained demand.

Gross margin recovered toward prior peak levels as Blackwell manufacturing yields improved progressively through the quarter, reducing the initial manufacturing costs from the production ramp and confirming the expected margin recovery trajectory.

We initiated a new share repurchase authorization of 25 billion dollars reflecting confidence in our long-term business trajectory and commitment to returning capital to shareholders while maintaining investment capacity.

Operating expenses grew at a slower rate than revenue as scale benefits offset continued research and development investment in next-generation GPU architectures, networking technology, and software platforms for the artificial intelligence ecosystem.

Item 3. Other Information
